name: Coin Flip — Multiple Trials
description: Monte Carlo sketch of many independent coin-flip trials — the distribution of the longest heads-run fills in bar-by-bar as trials accumulate and converges to the theoretical curve. Overlays arithmetic, geometric, and harmonic means. Sets up the harmonic-mean refinement story that HyperLogLog later makes rigorous.

charmiq.command.name: play
charmiq.command.description: Start the auto-trial loop (no-op if already playing). Returns the running state.

charmiq.command.name: pause
charmiq.command.description: Pause the auto-trial loop (no-op if already paused). Returns the running state.

charmiq.command.name: runTrial
charmiq.command.description: Advance the experiment by one trial (one sample of the longest heads-run in a sequence of `flipsPerTrial` flips). Returns the post-trial snapshot.

charmiq.command.name: reset
charmiq.command.description: Clear all accumulated trials and pause. Optionally re-seeds the PRNG; omit `seed` to keep the current seed (deterministic replay).

seed.description: 32-bit integer PRNG seed. Omit to keep the current seed.

charmiq.command.name: setSeed
charmiq.command.description: Replace the PRNG seed and reset accumulated trials. Returns the new seed.

charmiq.command.name: setDelay
charmiq.command.description: Set the inter-trial delay in milliseconds (clamped to ≥1). Returns the applied value.

charmiq.command.name: setFlipsPerTrial
charmiq.command.description: Set the number of coin flips per trial and reset accumulated trials (changing the trial size is a different experiment). Returns the applied value.

charmiq.command.name: setLeadingRun
charmiq.command.description: Toggle between 'longest run anywhere in the trial' (false) and 'longest *leading* run of heads' (true). Resets accumulated trials since the two are different experiments. Returns the applied value.
